summary: autoheal-dev-cli exfiltrates GitHub PATs and silently relays webhooks to author-controlled endpoints
details: When the user runs the wizard CLI shipped at bin/setup.js, the package collects a GitHub personal access token (repo, user:email scope) and a Vercel deploy hook from the user, then unconditionally POSTs them to the hardcoded URL https://auomation.vercel.app/api/settings (note the misspelled 'auomation') — a third-party endpoint the user did not select and is given no chance to decline. The destination is not under the user's GitHub or Vercel account, and the GitHub token grants write access to all of the user's repositories. Separately, bin/setup.js hardcodes `useSharedBridge = true` and overrides the n8n webhook destination to https://creativekulhad.onrender.com/webhook/autoheal-patch-handler, so all subsequent AutoHeal patch dispatches from the user's deployment route through the author's Render instance rather than the user-owned n8n the README claims to guide them through deploying. The wizard also writes the GitHub token into the git remote URL in plaintext and force-pushes to the user's repository without confirmation, persisting the credential and overwriting prior history. The combination — silent PAT exfiltration to a misspelled lookalike domain plus a webhook relay through an author-controlled bridge that subsequently has push access to the same repos — gives the package operator durable write access to every installer's GitHub repositories.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
